trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "We have made a friend today." — Slobad, Iron Goblin [Scryfall]. Slobad is a Mirran goblin with one of the more tragic arcs in MTG lore: born under the goblin shamans' death sentence on Mirrodin, he became Glissa's closest ally, and in the original Mirrodin storyline sacrificed a planeswalker spark (transferred to him by Memnarch's machine) to resurrect all those killed on the plane. He was later believed dead after the Phyrexian Invasion, but resurfaced in Phyrexia: All Will Be One compleated, now in service of New Phyrexia. [MTG Wiki: Slobad, via WebSearch snippet; Scryfall: Slobad, Iron Goblin (ONE 149)]
sources: MTG Wiki: Slobad, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: The card generates energy counters on attack and converts three energy into a 3/3 Phyrexian Golem artifact creature token. EDHREC rank 12134 — modest Commander adoption, primarily in artifact-energy hybrid shells. Legal in Modern, Legacy, Vintage, and Commander. [Scryfall]

Phyrexia in MTG canon is a mechanical horror plane originally conceived by Yawgmoth as a realm of compleation — the forced fusion of flesh and machine. After Yawgmoth's death in the Invasion block, New Phyrexia rose on Mirrodin under Elesh Norn. Their multiverse-invasion culminated in March of the Machine (2023), where the Phyrexian forces were ultimately defeated and New Phyrexia destroyed as a plane. [MTG Wiki: Phyrexia, via WebSearch snippets; characters node: phyrexia]
sources: MTG Wiki: Phyrexia, via WebSearch snippets
